runtime_policy: repo-only continuity status; does not launch Clash95, CDB, wrappers, PowerShell harnesses, or visible windows
guard_policy: save/load, turn, and campaign continuity remain blocked until a compact approved proof manifest documents an isolated test-save workflow, stable stage/candidate identity shared by every continuity lane, state hashes, route observations, and no live save mutation

save_load_roundtrip.title: Safe save/load roundtrip continuity
save_load_roundtrip.next_probe: run an approved isolated save/load roundtrip after short-tier stability

proof_summary.route: natural menu load slot 0 -> world-map poll 0040B0C3 -> forced saveGame(7) | second run: natural menu load slot 7 -> forced saveGame(8) -> byte compare
proof_summary.before_observation: save/7.dat written in the isolated workdir, 586414 bytes (16-byte name header + 586398-byte gameData), sha256 91dea97390668b4ea81a59cad6c9e37b3b283d8ef88f4e47dfb6ae3a64a4b979
proof_summary.after_observation: save/8.dat written after the natural slot-7 reload, byte-identical to 7.dat (same sha256), cmp exit 0; C:/Clash/save hashes unchanged against the pre-session baseline
proof_summary.summary: Hidden-CDB save->load->save roundtrip on the stable candidate: run A loads slot 0 via the game's natural menu route and force-calls saveGame(7); run B naturally loads slot 7 back through the load menu and force-calls saveGame(8). save/7.dat and save/8.dat are byte-identical (sha256 91dea973...), proving the full 586398-byte gameData block survives the load path with zero state drift.

turn_advancement.title: Turn advancement without state desync
turn_advancement.next_probe: run an approved deterministic turn-advance route after save/load is safe

proof_summary.route: natural menu load slot 0 -> world-map poll 0040B0C3 -> forced saveGame(9) -> forced next-player 0040AA60 -> forced saveGame(10)
proof_summary.before_observation: save/9.dat snapshot with player 0 active, sha256 7f13ba29e129709d2b5dd485d23e9d9aaab3e4b865b50bd879195382f14c7dd0
proof_summary.after_observation: save/10.dat snapshot after the engine-owned player advance to player 1, sha256 cd30bde1059618bb1fb6ffdd90193a4a23799295fc62d3adcd313909f4a2915d (differs from 9.dat as a turn change requires); C:/Clash/save hashes unchanged
proof_summary.summary: Hidden-CDB turn advance on the stable candidate: snapshot saveGame(9) with player 0 active, force-call the engine's own next-player routine 0040AA60, snapshot saveGame(10) after it returns with dword_5202EC advanced to player 1 (day_owner=1). Snapshots differ as a real turn change requires; the run stays healthy through the subsequent surface dump.

campaign_routes.title: Representative campaign route continuity
campaign_routes.next_probe: run a representative campaign route after short and medium tiers are stable

proof_summary.route: natural menu load slot 0 -> world-map poll -> saveGame(11) -> 610s redraw-driven route with 3 forced next-player advances (0040AA60) -> saveGame(12) -> base-template surface dump
proof_summary.before_observation: save/11.dat snapshot at route start (player 0), sha256 702e82a586133c510ec56ea3b1790a317fcbc4dcc3c37d793228109b04233889, written 08:16:16
proof_summary.after_observation: save/12.dat snapshot after 610s of play and 3 turn advances (player 3), sha256 90e676a4bc48268a1234011ac1609f4008d3ca99f75bb42b248c49326ec00b28, written 08:26:26; differs from 11.dat; C:/Clash/save hashes unchanged against the pre-session baseline
proof_summary.summary: Hidden-CDB 610-second campaign route on the stable candidate: snapshot saveGame(11) at route start, then pump the world map continuously via the redraw hook, advancing the engine's own next-player routine three times (players 0->1->2->3, a full human+AI progression) before snapshotting saveGame(12) at the 39040-tick mark. The game stays healthy for the whole route and the closing surface dump renders correctly; start and end snapshots differ as sustained play requires.
